questions.text: The presenter says that human beings tend to ‘flock’, or move towards people who are similar to them. Can you think of any examples of this from your own experience?

He talks about an experiment in a business, where employees who had the best ideas tended to bridge gaps (structural holes) between social networks. What does this show about creativity and innovation?
How do modern internet tools, such as social search, prioritise user desire over cognitive diversity?
What’s the speaker’s stance on ‘fixing’ the internet if we don’t like the way it works? Do you agree?
